human_verification.notes: Reviewed the child-care operations package for authority-source quality, child-care specificity, operational-state control, closure evidence, refusal posture, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to child-care operations execution, evidence handling, and routing across classrooms, transportation, inspections, incidents, and site readiness.
Authority sources cover official child-care licensing, monitoring, training, background-check, transportation, supervision, complaint, and safety controls with refresh and decay metadata.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing readiness evidence, inspection rework, unsupported completion, unauthorized release, licensing overreach, staffing handoff, source conflict, tenant adaptation, safety escalation, and systems-of-record conflict.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/child-care-and-family-services/child-care-operations-specialist/ as the canonical NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around licensing, staffing approvals, child release, child-protection escalation, legal interpretation, HR action, and clinical authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated child-care operations overlay where licensing, HR, child-protection, clinical, legal, and executive authority remain external.
The package is intentionally specification-based for runtime until a separately approved retained implementation is promoted.
